file_insights.impact: Critical UX improvement - guides users to dispatch newly created cards
file_insights.lines_modified: Approximately 2-5 lines (exact count unavailable due to truncated diff)

file_insights.impact: Human-readable progress tracking for project continuity and handoff documentation
file_insights.lines_modified: Approximately 50 lines added across multiple session entries

file_insights.impact: Enables session continuity and prevents duplicate work across multi-session implementations
file_insights.lines_modified: Approximately 40 lines (3 new subtask entries with attempt details)

patterns_discovered.pattern: Session-based execution model with persistent memory
patterns_discovered.significance: Each session (6→7→8→9) maps to one subtask (2-1→2-2→2-3→3-1). Attempt history and build commits prevent reruns. Session number deterministically tracks continuation.
patterns_discovered.evidence: Session 9 immediately followed Session 8; commit hash recorded in build_commits.json, attempt logged in attempt_history.json

patterns_discovered.significance: Four separate JSON files track different facets: plan status, attempt outcomes, git commits, and task logs. Requires coordinated updates to maintain consistency.
patterns_discovered.evidence: All four files updated in parallel; timestamps align to same session (2026-03-07T04:59:26)

patterns_discovered.pattern: Phased incremental delivery with explicit subtask boundaries
patterns_discovered.significance: Phase 1 (colors, subtasks 1-1 through 1-4) completed; Phase 2 (stats, subtasks 2-1 through 2-3) now complete; Phase 3 (UX messaging, subtasks 3-x) active. Each subtask has success criteria and verification steps.
patterns_discovered.evidence: Session 9 implementing subtask-3-1 (success message), directly follows completed subtask-2-3

patterns_discovered.pattern: User-facing messaging integrated into CLI after core features
patterns_discovered.significance: Color system and stats completed first (Phases 1-2); user guidance messages added in Phase 3. Indicates deliberate separation: implement features → add UX polish.
patterns_discovered.evidence: Subtask-3-1 success message added only after stats display confirmed working

patterns_discovered.pattern: Verification via cargo commands embedded in acceptance criteria
patterns_discovered.significance: Each subtask specifies explicit verification: `cargo test`, `cargo build`, grep for patterns in output. Prevents incomplete implementations and regression.
patterns_discovered.evidence: All previous subtasks (2-1, 2-2, 2-3) show 'All X tests pass' notes

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Truncated git diff obscures exact implementation details
gotchas_discovered.context: Session data diff is truncated; actual lines changed in main.rs cannot be fully verified
gotchas_discovered.implication: Code review and complexity assessment is partially blind. Cannot verify message formatting, color codes, or exact wording of success message.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always request full diff or read the source file directly; do not rely on truncated metadata alone

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Multiple metadata files must stay synchronized across sessions
gotchas_discovered.context: 4 JSON files + 1 TXT file updated in single commit. Timestamp coordination required.
gotchas_discovered.implication: Manual updates risk inconsistency: commit hash in build_commits.json but missing from attempt_history.json, or timestamp drift. If one file is stale, session recovery fails.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Automate metadata updates; consider single source-of-truth for session state. Use structured locking during multi-file updates.

gotchas_discovered.context: Session 9 → subtask-3-1; Session 8 → subtask-2-3, etc. Pattern holds but not enforced by system.
gotchas_discovered.implication: If a session implements multiple subtasks or skips one, metadata becomes ambiguous. Recovery tools cannot reliably map commits to work items.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Enforce or document the 1:1 contract. Add validation: each session number appears exactly once in attempt_history.json.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Success message phrasing must match dispatch command syntax
gotchas_discovered.context: Message says 'bop dispatch <id>'; if dispatch command changes, message becomes misleading
gotchas_discovered.implication: Acceptance criteria may pass (message appears) but guidance may be wrong. Users run invalid commands based on stale help text.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add acceptance criterion: verify that the exact command in success message is valid; update success_message constants in tests when dispatch API changes.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Phase 3 (UX messaging) depends on Phase 1-2 completeness
gotchas_discovered.context: Success message for 'bop new' assumes card creation works; refers to 'bop dispatch <id>'
gotchas_discovered.implication: If Phase 1 or 2 is incomplete or breaks, Phase 3 acceptance tests will fail (card creation fails, no ID to dispatch). Requires end-to-end integration testing.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add integration test that creates card, captures ID from success message, and verifies 'bop dispatch <id>' accepts it.

approach_outcome.description: Add success message to bop new: '✓ Card created → bop dispatch <id> to run it'
approach_outcome.implementation_strategy: Added user-facing success message to CLI output after card creation. Message provides immediate feedback (checkmark ✓) and guidance on next action (dispatch command with card ID).
approach_outcome.verification_method: Acceptance criterion likely includes: 1) Manual verification of success message display on 'bop new' execution, 2) Verification that message contains exact wording '✓ Card created → bop dispatch' and ID placeholder, 3) Cargo build and test pass
approach_outcome.key_achievement: UX improvement: users receive actionable next-step guidance immediately after card creation, reducing cognitive load and support questions

recommendations.recommendation: Automate metadata file updates (attempt_history.json, build_commits.json, implementation_plan.json) via post-commit hook or CI pipeline
recommendations.rationale: Manual synchronization of 4 JSON + 1 TXT file is error-prone and creates maintenance burden. Timestamps drift, commit hashes are omitted or incorrect. Automation ensures consistency and frees human effort.
recommendations.implementation: Add pre-push git hook that validates: (1) latest commit hash recorded in build_commits.json, (2) attempt_history.json marked success if commit exists, (3) timestamps match within 5 seconds

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration test covering 'bop new' → capture success message → extract card ID → 'bop dispatch <id>' full round-trip
recommendations.rationale: Current subtask acceptance criteria may verify message appears but not that the guidance is actionable. If dispatch command changes signature, users will be misguided. Integration test prevents this.
recommendations.implementation: Test should: (1) run `bop new --prompt 'test'` (2) parse success message regex `Card created.*dispatch ([a-f0-9-]+)` (3) verify extracted ID is valid (4) call `bop dispatch <id>` and confirm no error

recommendations.recommendation: Document the 1:1 session-to-subtask contract in CONTRIBUTING.md or project README
recommendations.rationale: Future developers (or AI agents) may not understand why session numbers match subtask IDs. Explicit contract prevents confusion and enables reliable automation.
recommendations.implementation: Add section: 'Session Continuity Model: Each session number corresponds to exactly one subtask ID. Session 9 implements subtask-3-1, session 10 implements subtask-3-2, etc. Metadata files record this mapping.'

recommendations.recommendation: Review actual wording of success message in main.rs against user research or UX guidelines (not yet verified in truncated diff)
recommendations.rationale: Message format affects user behavior. If it says 'bop dispatch' but command is actually 'bop run' or 'bop exec', users will be confused. Verify exact grammar, emoji usage (✓ vs ✅), and command accuracy.
recommendations.implementation: Read crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs lines around 'Card created'; compare success_message constant against dispatch command signature in main.rs

recommendations.recommendation: Add session insights JSON file for session 9 (similar to session_006.json, session_007.json, session_008.json)
recommendations.rationale: Sessions 6, 7, 8 have detailed session_insights JSON files in memory/. Session 9 should follow the same pattern for consistency and future analysis.
recommendations.implementation: Create .auto-claude/specs/018-cli-ux-colors-stats/memory/session_insights/session_009.json with structure: session_number, timestamp, subtasks_completed, discoveries (file_insights, patterns, gotchas, approach_outcome, recommendations)
